title: bidens edentula
intro: Bidens edentula, also known as toothless beggar-ticks, is a perennial plant that belongs to the aster family and is native to North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The plant's leaves and stems have been used to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems, and its extracts have been shown to have antioxidant and antimicrobial activities. Bidens edentula can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and it is often used in herbal remedies to promote overall health and well-being.
